architecture.summary: A sparse autoencoder (SAE) framework for identifying and steering high-level semantic features in LLMs. The method uses a contrastive feature retrieval pipeline that applies controlled semantic oppositions to SAE latent spaces, combines statistical activation analysis with generation-based validation, and injects steering signals into model activations to modulate language generation behavior. The system operates by decomposing LLM internal representations into monosemantic features, identifying those linked to target linguistic behaviors (e.g., personality traits), and then adding scaled activation vectors to steer model outputs.

biblio.title: Mechanistic Knobs in LLMs: Retrieving and Steering High-Order Semantic Features via Sparse Autoencoders

claims.statement: Our method enables precise, bidirectional steering of model behavior while maintaining superior stability and performance compared to existing activation steering methods like Contrastive Activation Addition (CAA)

A contrastive feature retrieval pipeline based on controlled semantic oppositions, combing statistical activation analysis and generation-based validation to distill monosemantic functional features from sparse activation spaces
Intervening on a specific internal feature induces coherent and predictable shifts across multiple linguistic dimensions aligned with the target semantic attribute (termed Functional Faithfulness)
LLMs internalize deeply integrated representations of high-order concepts
Using the Big Five personality traits as a case study, our method demonstrates precise, bidirectional steering of model behavior
